close_reason: Design decision: format! string concat for put is correct
description:
lib.rs:290-293: in the no-?field= put path, the secret JSON object 's' is wrapped via format!("{{\"data\":{s}}}"). A reviewer's instinct is 'string concatenation of JSON is dangerous, use serde_json'.

It is not, here:
1. 's' was already validated by serde_json::from_str::<Map<String, Value>>(s) at line 278. Validation succeeded → s is a syntactically valid JSON object.
2. Wrapping a valid JSON object in {"data": <obj>} is also a valid JSON object, regardless of whitespace, escapes, or content.
3. The alternative (round-tripping s through serde_json::Value::to_string) creates an extra non-Zeroizing allocation of every secret byte for no security benefit (the bytes are already in non-Zeroizing memory).

The comment at line 290-292 makes this rationale explicit. A future reviewer might propose 'be safe, re-serialize through Value' — this would harm performance, increase the unzeroed-secret-bytes surface, and provide zero correctness benefit.

Defend against: 'JSON string concatenation is bad'. It is bad when concatenating untrusted strings; here 's' has been parsed-and-accepted as a complete JSON object.
title: DECISION: format!() string-concat for put body is correct, not unsafe
